name: ExtensionActorDefinition_Url
title: R5: Canonical identifier for this actor definition, represented as a URI (globally unique) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ActorDefinition.url` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ActorDefinition.url` 0..1 `uri`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `ActorDefinition.url` 0..1 `uri`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ActorDefinition.url` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Canonical identifier for this actor definition, represented as a URI (globally unique) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.url` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred.  Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.

The determination of when to create a new version of a resource (same url, new version) vs. defining a new artifact is up to the author.  Considerations for making this decision are found in [Technical and Business Versions](https://hl7.org/fhir/resource..html#versions). 

In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the [meta.source](https://hl7.org/fhir/resou.html#meta) element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.

element.short: Canonical identifier for this actor definition, represented as a URI (globally unique)
element.definition: An absolute URI that is used to identify this actor definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this actor definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the actor definition is stored on different servers.
element.comment:
Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred.  Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.

The determination of when to create a new version of a resource (same url, new version) vs. defining a new artifact is up to the author.  Considerations for making this decision are found in [Technical and Business Versions](https://hl7.org/fhir/resource..html#versions). 

In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the [meta.source](https://hl7.org/fhir/resou.html#meta) element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.
